def: adv. & s.m. Additional, extra, unusual, extraordinary; extraneous; general; — in addition to, over and above; — anything extra or additional; extra tax; a horse that is led for show; — s.f. That which is on the surface; cream ( = malāʼī)

Is naukrī meṅ kuchh bālāī yāft bhī hai? 2. Bribes ( rishvat ); illegal gratifications. Kachehrī kī naukrī meṅ bālāī paidā bahut hai . There are nice pickings to be had in the Courts.
(A led horse); adj. Over and above, extraordinary. بالائي مزي bālā-ī maze, s. plur. Secret pleasures. بالائي يافت bālā-ī yāft, s. f. Perquisites.

def: adj. Hairy; shaggy. bāl denā , v. n. lit . To give one's hair. 1. To be shaved preparatory to performing the obsequies of the dead—a Hindu rite especially incumbent on the son. 2. E. To cause the hair of the head to fall forward and backward before the tāziah of the Moharrum—a Mohamedan rite performed by women who bend forwards and backwards in the act while they beat their breasts. bāl rakhnā, yā baṛhānā , v. n. To let the hair grow. Kapṛe raṅge, bāl baṛhāë, ban baiṭhe haiṅ jogī jī . Song. His raiment dyed and hair grown long, a jogī made he takes his seat. bāl. sufaid honā , v. n. To become grey (the hair). bāl-safā, safā-chaṭ , n. m. Slang. An orpiment used by prostitutes for destroying the pubes ( nūrā ). bāl-kamā'nī , n. f. The hair spring. bāl kī bheṛ, yā kammal banānā, bāt kā batak- kaṛ k., rāī kā parbat k., suī kā phāvṛā k., par kā kāg banānā , v. n. lit . To make a sheep or a blanket with one hair, spin a long yarn, make a mountain from a mustard seed, a spade from a needle, or a crow from a feather. To multiply words; exaggerate; make a mountain of a molehill. bāl kī khāl kheṅch'nā, yā nikālnā , v. n. To split hairs; to inspect very minutely; to be too critical. bāl lochan , n. m. The act of plucking the hair. bāl noch'nā , v. n. To pull or pluck the hair. chinḍāl bāl , n. m. A hair on the forehead (considered unlucky). kāle bāl , n. m. Wom. The pubes ( jhāṅṭ ). kān-bāl , n. m. The Hindu ceremony of shaving a child's head for the first time ( mūṅḍan ) before boring his ears ( kan-chhedan ). ghuṅgrāle bā, ghūṅgar-wāle bāl , n. m. from H. ghūṅgrū . Curly hair; woolly hair. bāloṅ choṭoṅ , n. m. A game at marbles.

Yāro, suno yeh dadh ke luṭaīyā kā bāl-pan, Aur Madhpurī nagar ke basaīyā kā bāl-pan, Mohan sarūp nirt-karaīyā kā bāl-pan, Ban ban ke guāl, gaūeṅ-charaīyā kā bāl-pan, Aisā thā bāṅsrī ke bajaīyā kā bāl-pan. Kyā kyā kahūṅ maiṅ Krishn Kanhaīyā kā bāl-pan! On the childhood of Krishn. Nazīr. Now listen, friends, it is the story of the milk-thief's childhood, Of him who dwelt in Madhypuri erst I tell the childhood, Of him whose face all charmed and dance entranced, I sing the childhood, Of cowherd-swain who did his cows in forests graze, the childhood, Of dulcet notes which from his pipe he poured, the master's childhood. more: s. m. The name of a grass.

def: adj. Like, resembling.
s.m. Ant-hill; snake's hole (cf. bāṅbī).
s.m. Hole from which water springs up, source, fount (cf. bam).
v.n. To vomit; — v.t. To vomit up or forth.
s.m. Dwarf; the fifth or dwarf-incarnation of the god Vishnu (undertaken to humble the pride of the Daitya Bali, who had acquired dominion over the three worlds).

s.f. = bāmhnī, q.v. s.f. Wife of a Brāhmaṇ, &c. (see brāhmaṇī); a kind of lizard; — a medicinal herb, the moon-plant, Aselepias acida; — the yellow stamens of the lotus (in contradistinction to ćamārī, 'black stamens of a flower'); a stye on the eye-lid, or a disease which causes the eyelashes to fall off.
more: n. f. S. ब्राह्मणी The wife of a Bāman. Kyoṅ na ho bāmnī bahut taīyār, Woh to khātī hai rāt din sidhā . Mazāq. So plump and round the Brāhmnī grows, For night and day rich food she stows.

n. f. 1. A species of lizard (E. loṭan ). 2. A disease of the eye which causes the eyelashes to drop. 3. The yellow stamens of the lotus flower as distinguished from chamārī the black stamens of a flower. Hin. 4. A medicinal plant or herb; the moonplant.

def: s.f. Wailing, lamentation; — intj. Alas!
subs: v.n. To bewail the dead, wail, lament (in the Hindū fashion, viz. by uttering loud cries or wails and slapping the mouth with the hand at the same time).

def: s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts (of low repute) in the Doʼāb.
adv. On a mere, simple, or single (order, glance, &c.); on the instant (of), instantly, immediately, forthwith.
adv. By reason (of, -ke), on account (of), in pursuance (of); in accordance (with), according (to), in conformity (with); as per.
adv. According to the requirements of, by the exaction of; in consequence of, conformable to; through.
adj. Civil; courteous; urbane; affable; kind; obliging; humane.
adv. In the lapse (of); ago.

s.f. Quality, property, temper, disposition, nature, character, conduct, manners, habit, custom (cf. ān-bān, s.v. ān); — s.m. The marriage rite which prescribes a certain number of baths (generally from three to eleven) for the bride and bridegroom
more: In Compos. A keeper, a driver, as dar-bān door-keeper. Jiske peshe meṅ bān, uskā gurū Shaitān . Prov. Javāb. " Hāṅ, mehr-bān ." Whose trade has a bān has priest is Shaitān (Satan). Ans. "True, mehr-bāṅ " (kind Sir). ☞ Gāṛī-bān cart-driver, hāthī-bān elephant-driver, etc. are considered great rascals. bān n. m. The name of a tree; Hyperanthera morunga, Melia sempervirens ; that which yields benzoin ( Styrax Benzoin ); a tree the leaves and flowers of which are * Double entendres in capping verses called Kabīr . sweet-scented, called by the Persians bed mushk .

n. m. H. ban make; bun , S. वे weave, hence S. वर्ण quality, Pr. vaṇṇo . 1. A rope made of mūṅj , q. v. Sone kī woh nār kahāwe, Binā kasauṭī bān dikhāwe . Saudā. Riddle. Ans. khāṭ a bed. 1. Gold manifest the lady's name, Unproved by touchstone claims acclaim. 2. The thing you sleep on is she called, Without being strung a string it's called. 2. ( bānī , n. f.) Nature; character; quality ( subhāo ). 3. ( bānī , n. f.) Habit ( ādat ); custom; practice; wont ( sahej ); way; manner; disposition; a second nature. 1. Koī baṭṭoṅ se muṅh bhī toṛegā, Apnī bānī magar na chhoṛegā . Wom. Shauq. Though your mouth with great weights one may hammer, Your bad habit you'll ne'er give up, shammer. 2. Tum kāṭo mere nāk aur kānī, Maiṅ nā chhoṛūṅ apnī banī . Prov. You may cut off my nose and my ear, My habit I'll ne'er give up, ne'er. 3. Kyā bān paṛī, saīyāṅ, torī, mo se khelan āë horī, Lāl gulāl ke bādar ch…

1. s. f. Temper, quality, manners. (Sans. वर्ण Kind, quality). 2. (or vāṇ वाण ) s. m. An arrow; a rocket used in battle. 3. The name of an Asur, son of Rājā Bali. 4. (for वान ) The high tide in the Indian rivers commonly called the Bore. 5. A kind of rope made of مونج mūnj, used to form the bottoms of beds as well as for other purposes.

s. m. 1. Name of a tree, سهجنا sahajnā (Guilandina moringa) the seeds of which are called ḥabbu-l-bān, Ben-nut; or, according to some, بکاين bakāyan (Melia sempervirens). 2. Name of the tree which yields Benzoin (Styrax Benzoe). 3. A tree, the leaves and flowers of which are sweet-scented, called by the Persians Bed-mushk.
(used as an affix in compos. only) Keeper, guardian; so, دربان dar-bān, Door-keeper; سگبان sag-bān, Dog-keeper.
s. A grenade, a common rocket. آسماني چکربان āsmānī chakkar bān and بهوٴين چکربان bhūʼīṅ chakkar bān, Sorts of fireworks: see Qanoon-e-islam, app. p. lviii.
subs: v.n. To take the baths prescribed by the marriage rite (mentioned above)
v.n. To fall into, or to acquire or form, a habit, &c.; to be trained; to be accustomed (to)
v.n. To lay the foundation of character, &c., cultivate a habit, &c.; to educate, train; to habituate, accustom, inure; to naturalize

def: s.m. Reed-shaft, arrow, dart; rocket (for battle), sky-rocket; — the bloody track of a wounded animal; — name of an Asur (son of Rājā Bali, an enemy of Vishṇu)
s.m. The high tidal wave in Indian rivers commonly called 'the Bore.'

ety: S. वान and वाण, rt. वे
def: s.m. Rope made of twisted grass (ḍāb), or of mūṅj (used for the bottom of beds as well as for other purposes).
A suffix signifying 'keeper,' 'man,' &c. (e.g. dar-bān, 'door-keeper'; gāṛī-bān, 'cart-man'; bāg̠-bān, 'garden-er.' The suffix is also written wān).

s.m. The ben-tree, Hyperanthera moringa (syn. sahajnā); — the tree Melia sempervirens (syn. bakāʼin); — the tree which yields Benzoin or Benjamin, Styrax benzoin; — a species of willow, Salix Ӕgyptia (Pers. syn. bed-mushk).

s.f. 1˚ = bāṅh; 2˚ adj. = bām, q.q.v. s.f. The arm (from the elbow to the shoulder); sleeve; support, help; supporter, protector; security, guarantee (as when a person trusts himself in the power of an enemy, a third person, who engages to return him to his home, &c. in security, is his bāṅh); power, strength
subs: lit. 'To have the arm raised'; to be in a position of power, &c.; to be powerful
s.m. Strength of arm; support, ally; a younger brother; — bāṅh-balī, adj. & s.m. Strong in the arm; — one who is strong in the arm
bāṅh gahnā (-kī), lit. 'To hold the arm (of)'; to aid, protect, support
(-kī), To lose one's prop, support, protector, &c.; to be destitute of friends or protectors
(-par), To tuck up the sleeves (for, or against); to get ready (for)
(-ko), To lend an arm (to), to support, assist, aid.

def: s.m. (f. ?), Forest, jungle, wood, grove, copse, brake; cotton-field; cotton crop
more: n. m. banī , n. f. S. वन a wood. 1. Forest; jungle; a wood. 2. A cotton field; cotton crop ( bāṅgā ). W. banobās , Pop. n. m. S. वनवास Banishment; exile. ban-bāsī , n. m. An inhabitant of the wood. ban-bilāo , n. m. A wild cat. ban-tītar , n. m. Sand-grouse. ban-jātrā , n. f. A pilgrimage through the 84 koses of Brij. land. ban-jātrī , n. A pilgrim who makes the above pilgrimage. ban-char , n. 1. A wild man or animal; a monkey. 2. A forester; woodman. ban-devtā , n. m. The god of forests. ban-saṭī , n. f. The wood of the cotton tree used for fuel, and in the sides of grain carts.
(contract. of ابن ibn, Heb. ) A son.

s. f. A forest, a wood. بن جاترا ban-jātrā, s. f. The pilgrimage through the 84 forests of Braja. بن جهاڙنا ban-jhāṛnā or بن مارنا ban-mārnā or بن مجهاڙنا ban-majhāṛnā, To beat the forests. بن کنڐا ban-kanḍā or بن گونٿها ban-gonṭhā (Sans. वनगोविट् ) Cow-dung found in the forests.
subs: (S. vana+vāsa), bano-bās, s.m. Living in a wood, residence in a forest; wild or unsettled manner of life; — banishment, exile; — one who lives in a wood, &c. ( = next)
adj. & s.m. Dwelling in a forest or wood; dweller in woods, inhabitant of a forest, forester; hermit, anchorite; — s.f. Life of a hermit
s.m. ban-billī, s.f. (S. vana+viḍāla), A kind of wild cat, Felis caracal
(S. vana+vihāra), s.m. Wandering or sporting in the woods or forests
(S. vana+bhaṇṭākī), s.m. The wild egg-plant, Solanum melongena
adj. & s.m. Retiring into a forest, leading the life of an anchorite; — hermit, anchorite
s.m.f. Forest animal, wild animal, wild beast
ban-turoʼi, s.f. A wild species of the Luffa (or Cucumis) acutangula
s.m. Name of a class of people who formerly acted as wood-rangers in Gorakhpur
(S. vana+yātrā), s.f. Pilgrimage through the eighty-four forests of the Braj country
s.m. A pilgrim who makes the above pilgrimage
v.n. To beat the forests or woods
adj. & s.m. Moving or living in a forest or wood, sylvan, wild, savage; — any creature, or wild animal, that inhabits the jungle; monkey; forester woodman; wild man, savage
s.f. A kind of high jungle grass of which elephants are very fond
s.f. Wood of the cotton tree (used for fuel, and in the sides of grain-carts)
s.f. Fee exacted by the landlord from the cultivator for permission to collect the juice of the forest date-trees
ban-kaṭṭī, s.f. Fee paid for cutting timber in a forest; the right obtained by clearing a jungle and bringing it under cultivation; cleared land
s.m. Dried cow-dung found in forests (and used as fuel)
s.m. Produce of jungle or forest lands (e.g. gum, honey, &c.); revenue from woods and forests
s.m. Grass used in making ropes
s.m. Land on which cotton has grown during the past season
s.m. Name of a forest in India
(S. vana+makshikā), s.m. Gad-fly; the jungle bee, wild-honey bee
ban-mālā, s.f. A garland of wild flowers (usually of the tulsī, the kundā, the mandār, the pārijāt, and the saro-ruhā or lotus), reaching down to the feet; the chaplet worn by Kr̤ishṇa
adj. & s.m. Having or wearing a ban-māl; one who wears a ban-māl; an epithet of Kr̤ishṇa
(S. vana+mānusha), s.m. Wild man of the woods; the ourang-outang; the Lemur tardigradus
v.n. To traverse the forest, &c. = ban-jhāṛnā, q.v.
(S. vana+utsarga), s.m. Name of a ceremony (much resembling a marriage ceremony) performed in honour of a newly-planted orchard, and without which it is not proper to partake of its fruit
s.f. A female residing in a forest or jungle.

def: part. (of bannā) = bankar, q.v. s.f. Quality, property, temper, disposition, nature, character, conduct, manners, habit, custom (cf. ān-bān, s.v. ān); — s.m. The marriage rite which prescribes a certain number of baths (generally from three to eleven) for the bride and bridegroom
subs: v.n. To take the baths prescribed by the marriage rite (mentioned above)
v.n. To fall into, or to acquire or form, a habit, &c.; to be trained; to be accustomed (to)
v.n. To lay the foundation of character, &c., cultivate a habit, &c.; to educate, train; to habituate, accustom, inure; to naturalize

def: v. a. To prate idly, to talk foolishly.

ety: S. वन; or perhaps the rt. of bunnā, q.v.
def: s.f. Price paid for pasturage; remuneration for harvesting (usually one bundle out of a certain fixed number); quantity of grain given for a day's weeding, or for other daily labour (usually two and a half or three seers; — syn. bannī, banī).

s.m. The woof (as opp. to tānā, 'warp'); a silken thread used in weaving and sewing, &c.; a kind of hemispherical bucket made of iron plates closely riveted together, used in raising water for irrigation.
more: n. m. S. वर्ण a covering, dress. 1. The woof, in contradistinction to tānā warp. Tānā nāche, bānā nāche, nāche sūt purānā . Play. Chaubolā . The warp and woof in sprightly dance, The old thread (woman), see, like a young one prance. 2. A silken thread used in weaving and sewing, etc. 3. Dress; garment; costume; mode of dress. Saīyāṅ, chuṛyān pahan le, kar le janānā bhek, Apnā bānā mohe de, tū khaṛā tamāsā dekh . Play. Put on my bangles, spouse, and don a woman's dress, Give me your garments male, look on the game scatheless. (Said to a man who had shown the white feather). 4. A badge; uniform; livery ( wardī ). 5. Appearance; form; shape; colour. 6. A sect or profession ( bhek ). 7. A war instrument; a kind of weapon. 8. A kind of hemispherical bucket made of iron plates closely riveted together, used in raising water for irrigation. bānā bāṅdhnā , v. n. 1. To be equipped, accoutred, arm…

s. m. 1. ( वर्ण Colour, class) Habit, profession. 2. Fashion in dress, peculiar to individuals or to bodies taken individually (so, regimentals may be called bānā ); uniformity. 3. A kind of weapon. 4. A veil. 5. ( वाणि Weaving) The woof in weaving. باناباندهنا bānā bāndhnā, To be ready, to be determined.

def: s.m. A kind of rocket; a kind of sword, a sort of paṭṭā with a thick-edged blade and a wooden hilt
subs: v.n. To be armed; to be ready; to be determined

def: s.m. Stake, bet, wager
subs: v.n. To stake, bet, wager (syn. budnā).

def: s.m. Appearance, form, shape, colour; property, habit, peculiarity; profession, caste, sect, class; veil, covering; dress, garment, costume, fashion or style of dress; uniform, livery; mask, disguise
subs: v.n. To change (one's) dress; to disguise oneself (syn. bhes badalnā).

def: v.n. To open, unfold, streten, spread; to cast; — v.n. To open ( = khulnā); to burst open, split ( = phaṭnā).

s.m. = بانا bānā, q.v. s.m. The woof (as opp. to tānā, 'warp'); a silken thread used in weaving and sewing, &c.; a kind of hemispherical bucket made of iron plates closely riveted together, used in raising water for irrigation.
part. (of banānā), Having made, having prepared, &c. (see banānā; syn. banā-kar, banā-ke)
subs: v.t. To prepare, or get ready, and bring; to settle, manage, arrange, accomplish.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

def: adj. (f. -ī), Made, formed, done, got ready, prepared, constructed, composed; finished, completed; fixed, settled, established; well arranged, in good order; well-to-do, prosperous, wealthy; healthy, robust, strong, stout, plump; hoaxed, cajoled; alloyed, counterfeit, fictitious, spurious, forged; artificial, not real, sham, mock, false, invented, fabricated; — s.m. Bridegroom ( = bannā); darling; marriage song; — land dressed and ready for the seed
more: adj. 1. Made; formed; done; got up. 2. Settled; fixed; established. Banībāt hai . 3. Finished; completed; entire. 4. In good order; well set; well arranged. 5. Plump; healthy; robust; strong. 6. Wealthy; prosperous; happy ( rajā pujā ). 7. Artificial; sham; mock; not genuine ( naqlī ). 8. Alloyed; counterfeit; spurious; forged ( jālī ); fictitious. Banā huā sikkā . Counterfeit coin. 9. False; invented; got up. 10. Unfounded; unreasonable; groundless. 11. Imaginary; ideal; fabulous; mythical. banā ṭhanā , adj. Elaborately adorned; decked out; set off. Baṛā banā ṭhanā rahtā hai .
1. s. m. A bridegroom. 2. part. (of بنا bannā or بنانا) Made, prepared, having made, &c.
subs: adj. (f. banī-banāʼī), Ready made, finished, complete, perfect, entire; — banā-ṭhanā, adj. (f. banī-ṭhanī), Dressed out, got up, decked out, elaborately adorned ( = ban-ṭhan)
adj. (f. banī-ćunī), Arranged, set in order; decked out, &c. = banā-ṭhanā, q.v.

bane rahnā, v.n. To remain waiting, to wait, attend, remain in attendance, or at one's post; to continue, be maintained, remain unaltered, last long, be permanent, remain for ever; to do well, thrive, prosper, continue in good condition.
ex: انسان پانی کے بنا جی نہیں سکتے
wety: Inherited from Sauraseni Prakrit 𑀯𑀺𑀡𑀸 (viṇā), from Sanskrit विना (vínā). Doublet of وِنَا (vinā), a tatsama. Cognate with Punjabi ਬਿਨ (bin) / بِن (bin), Mewari वना (vanā) Kachchi વિણ- (viṇa-), Marathi बिन- (bin-).

def: On this account, therefore.
(A. pl. of P. bandar), s.m. pl. Ports, harbours.
s.m. = bannāphir, q.v. s.m. Name of a tribe of Rājpūts.
s.f. The violet, Viola odorata.
more: n. m. A violet. gul-i-banafshah , n. m. Violet flower.

def: 1. Essentially; intrinsically; personally; in propria persona (بذاته) 2. A priori .
s.f. = baniyāʼin, q.v.
more: s. f. Wife of a بنيا (shopkeeper, &c.) q. v. See بنياين.

def: s.m. = ban-kanḍā, q.v.s.v. ban.
more: s. m. Cow-dung dried for fuel (not made up). See under S. بن ban.

def: v.t. To trade, traffic ( = banaj karnā); to dispose of.
more: v. a. 1. To trade ( banaj k .). 2. To give in marriage; to marry. Mujhe abhī do beṭe banajne haiṅ. ban'jī , n. f. The business of a pedlar.

def: s.m. Agreement, engagement; opportunity, occasion, opening
more: n. m. Opportunity. See (اِتّفاق 8, 9, 10). Bānak banā to āëṅge .
n. f. The crowing of a cock; the Mah. call to prayer ( azāṅ ). bāṅg denā , v. n. To call Mahommedans to prayer; to crow (a cock). bāṅg-i-subah , n. f. The call to morning prayer.

ety: ban = S. वन+dim. aff. ak = S. क
def: s.f. A sort of fruit; a kind of satin; pimple; drops of sweat on the face.
adv. In the name (of); to the address (of).
adj. Left (hand) = bāmā, bāṅwāṅ, bāyāṅ, q.q.v.

v.t. To make, form, fashion, shape, mould, create, prepare, get ready, manufacture, construct, compose, build, fabricate, invent, do, perform, finish, complete; to repair, mend, rectify, adjust, adorn, decorate, deck, trim; to colour, paint, polish; to cause to agree, make harmonize, to reconcile; to pluck (a fowl); to dress or cook (food); to put on, assume (a look, air, &c.), to feign, pretend; to mock; to hoax
more: v. a. Caus. of بنا q. v. To paint; varnish; polish; gild. Baiṭhe haiṅ ādmī hī dukāneṅ lagā lagā, Aur ādmī hī phirte haiṅ rakh sir pe khonchā, Kahtā hai koī, "lo," koī kahtā hai "lā re, lā," Kis kis tarah se becheṅ haiṅ chīzeṅ banā banā, Aur mol le rahā hai so hai woh bhī ādmī . Ādmī-nāmah. Man. Nazīr. And they are men who sit in shops with goods laid out, And men they are who on their heads bear trays about, Some 'pray, come buy,' cry out, some 'bring it here' now shout, In various ways with things made up the eye is caught, And they are men by whom these things are being bought. naë sire, yā ḍaul se banānā ; Ped. az sar-i-nau banānā , v. a. To form anew; renew; reconstruct; rebuild.

(trans. of بننا) v. a. To make, prepare, form, fashion, shape, to do, fabricate, build, compose, perform, to make to agree, to adjust, adorn, reconcile, to pluck (a fowl), to dress (victuals), to mend, repair, invent, rectify, mock, feign.
subs: banā-lenā, v.t. intens. forms of, and = banānā (for banā-lānā, see s.v. past conj. part. banā).
wety: Urdu verb set بَنْنا (bannā) بَنانا (banānā) بَنْوانا (banvānā) Regular causative form of بننا (bannā).

def: v.n. See s.v. bannā. v.n. See بننا bannā.
v. n. To be performed or effected, to succeed, to be fortunate, to prosper.
(from بنانا) s. m. 1. Preparation, decoration, dressing, decking oneʼs self. 2. Concord, understanding, reconciliation. بنأوکرنا banāʼo karnā, To adorn, bedeck, decorate.

s.m. Preparation, formation, composition, fabric, &c. ( = banāwaṭ, q.v.); dressing, preparing, decking (oneself); decoration, embellishment, adornment; good understanding, harmony, concord, mutual agreement (of persons, things, or qualities), reconciliation; adjustment, amendment, correction, remedy
more: n. m. 1. Decoration; embellishment; adornment. Kyā kyā banāo karte haiṅ dam bhar ke wāste! Saīyad. 2. Correction ( islāh ); amendment; remedy; cure. banāo karnā , v. a. To adorn; deck; decorate. banāo siṅghār , n. m. Adornment ( banāo , 1).
subs: (-kā), To adorn, bedeck, ornament, decorate, embellish
(-se), A good understanding to exist (between or with), to be on good terms, get on well (together).

def: v.t. = banānā, q.v. v.t. To make, form, fashion, shape, mould, create, prepare, get ready, manufacture, construct, compose, build, fabricate, invent, do, perform, finish, complete; to repair, mend, rectify, adjust, adorn, decorate, deck, trim; to colour, paint, polish; to cause to agree, make harmonize, to reconcile; to pluck (a fowl); to dress or cook (food); to put on, assume (a look, air, &c.), to feign, pretend; to mock; to hoax
more: v. a. To make, to prepare. See بنانا banānā.
subs: banā-lenā, v.t. intens. forms of, and = banānā (for banā-lānā, see s.v. past conj. part. banā).

def: s.m. A Brāhmaṇ of the third order who has passed through the stages of student and householder and has left his house and family for the woods (syn. āsram); hermit, anchorite.
s. m. One who retires from the world with his wife and family, and passes his life in devotion in the forests; an anchoret or hermit. See آسرم.
s.m. Monkey = bandar, q.v.
s.f. The holy city Benares.
wety: Inherited from Prakrit 𑀯𑀸𑀭𑀸𑀡𑀲𑀻 (vārāṇasī), from Sanskrit वाराणसी (vārāṇasī) (which is a metathesized form). Alternatively, named after the Rājā Bānār, who had rebuilt the city.

def: v. a. S. वि+दृ to tear. 1. To cut; cut in pieces; slice; chop; multilate. 2. To cut off; behead; kill. Sīs terā maiṅ turat banārūṅ, apnā nāh kaṭāūṅgā. Daule se dūr na jāūṅgā . (The shepherd ( gaḍaryā ) to the cultivator ( kisān ) Your head I'll cut off straight, my own you shall not cut, Here from this boundary line I will not budge a foot.
s.m. lit. 'King of the wood'; a large forest tree; a large tree bearing fruit, but apparently having no blossoms (as several species of the fig, the jack-tree, &c.); tree (generally).
s.f. = banāspattī, q.v. s.f. Forest leaves; wild fruit; grass, herbage, vegetation, the vegetable kingdom.
